title: eriophorum scheuchzeri subsp. altaicum
intro: Eriophorum scheuchzeri subsp. altaicum, also known as the Altaic cotton grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Russia and other parts of Eastern Europe, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Altaic cotton grass include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and combat infections, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
